scenario: Helios Insurance handles 3500 claims per day in Camunda 8.8 across an adjusters team of 12 users. A user task must be visible to every team member and any one of them can claim it; only the user that claimed the task may complete it from Tasklist after that point.
question: Which Tasklist user task assignment configuration matches the described behaviour?

options.text: Set candidate groups to the adjusters group; any candidate may claim, which sets the assignee, and only the assignee can then complete
Set the assignee to the adjusters group as a comma-separated value; Tasklist treats group assignees as candidate-style assignments at runtime
Set the assignee to the first adjuster and let Tasklist rotate the assignee across the team automatically until a user completes the task
Set candidate users to the adjusters group as a comma-separated value of usernames; Tasklist auto-claims the task on first sign-in by any user

a.text: Correct. Camunda 8 user tasks use candidate groups (and candidate users) for visibility and an assignee for the actual owner; only the assignee can complete.

b.text: Incorrect. The assignee property accepts a single user reference, not a group; group visibility is the role of the candidate groups property.

c.text: Incorrect. Tasklist does not auto-rotate assignees; assignment changes only through explicit claim or reassign actions.

d.text: Incorrect. Candidate users accepts user names but never auto-claims a task on sign-in; a user must explicitly claim the task.

explanation: Tasklist uses candidate groups (and candidate users) for visibility and a single assignee for completion ownership, matching the team-with-claim pattern.
